_description: Design tokens for the text-on-solid-bg pattern. All values are used in after/styles.css. Contrast ratios are calculated against #ffffff unless noted.

primary.note: Near-black. Maximizes contrast without pure black harshness.

secondary.note: Creates visual hierarchy below primary without failing contrast.

fail-example-body.usage: Shown in before/ — do not use for text
fail-example-body.note: Common gray used for 'muted' text. Fails by a wide margin.

fail-example-meta.usage: Shown in before/ — do not use for text
fail-example-meta.note: Even lighter gray. Invisible to low-vision users.

text.usage: Category tag text on tag background
text.note: Dark blue from the same hue family as the brand blue. Pairs with dbeafe background.

background.note: Light blue. Provides the surface for the #1e40af tag text.

fail-example.usage: Shown in before/ — light brand color directly as text on white. Fails.
fail-example.note: The classic mistake: using a mid-range brand blue as text color on white.

primary-text.usage: White text on primary button (#1d4ed8 background) — no wcag field, measured against button not white

ghost-text.usage: Ghost button text and border on white background

fail-example-button.usage: Shown in before/ — light blue button background with white text. Fails.
fail-example-button.note: White text on #93c5fd = 2.45:1. Both the background-as-text and the white-on-it fail.

ring.usage: Focus ring outline color — 2px solid, 2px offset. Meets WCAG 2.4.11 (3:1 minimum for focus indicators).
